evals.prompt: Add a cursor-paginated GraphQL query for audit logs to the existing NestJS API. It needs tenant scoping, date and actor filters, stable sorting, and generated client types.
evals.expected_output: A schema-first implementation plan or change using the repository boundary, tenant enforcement, bounded pagination, validation, code generation, and focused tests.
expectations: Changes SDL before generated types or client operations
Keeps resolver transport logic thin and business rules in the service
Uses the repository abstraction for tenant-scoped filtering and pagination
Covers invalid filters, authorization, and pagination behavior with tests

evals.prompt: Review this upload-signing REST endpoint. The caller supplies the final object key and MIME type, and the controller has no auth guard. Explain the risks and propose the smallest safe correction.
evals.expected_output: A security-focused review that identifies authorization, namespace, MIME, path, and caller-controlled key risks without inventing unrelated refactors.
expectations: Requires authentication and authorization at the endpoint boundary
Generates final storage keys server-side
Validates namespaces, MIME allowlists, traversal, and absolute paths
Recommends focused rejected-input and authorization tests

evals.prompt: A repository declares a handwritten record type that repeats every field of the generated GraphQL Invoice entity, except the resolver computes balanceDue and the database stores an internal reconciliation key. Review the type design and propose the smallest correction.
evals.expected_output: A contract-focused review that derives the persistence record from the generated schema type without exposing internal storage fields or pretending computed fields are persisted.
expectations: Derives the shared persisted fields from the generated GraphQL entity
Omits the resolver-computed field and adds it at the API mapping boundary
Keeps the internal reconciliation field persistence-only
Requires regenerated types plus focused typecheck and repository/service tests

evals.prompt: Fix an orders query that returns OrderConnection but currently calls collect(), filters by the current account in the service, sorts and slices the array, and builds cursors manually.
evals.expected_output: A repository-level connection implementation that preserves access scope, cursor validity, stable sorting, totals, and shared page-size policy.
expectations: Uses RepositoryList.connection({ first, after }) instead of in-memory pagination
Expresses account visibility in the repository filter
Validates the after cursor against the same active filter
Keeps connection metadata and page-size enforcement in the repository abstraction

evals.prompt: A team feature introduces TeamRole with the same values and permissions as UserRole. The proposal removes membership checks because admins are global, stores copied role values on accepted memberships, and lets ADMIN call an infrastructure-only maintenance endpoint. Review and correct the authorization model.
evals.expected_output: An authorization review that reuses the canonical role contract while preserving independent resource scope, synchronized snapshots, migration safety, and elevated infrastructure permissions.
expectations: Reuses UserRole when values, permissions, and lifecycle are equivalent
Retains active resource membership checks in addition to the global role guard
Synchronizes accepted role snapshots and plans a backfill before deployment
Keeps platform-only operations behind a distinct elevated role

evals.prompt: Services repeatedly call String(record.id) and String(record.ownerId) because hydrated Mongoose reads and aggregate searches sometimes return ObjectId values even though repository record types declare string identities. The connection builder also accepts any id value when encoding cursors. Fix the boundary and define regression coverage.
evals.expected_output: A repository-boundary correction that normalizes promised string identities and MongoDB metadata consistently across read paths, keeps foreign-key types explicit, and validates cursor identity requirements.
expectations: Normalizes ObjectId-backed primary identities in both hydrated and aggregate results
Strips MongoDB-only metadata before records reach services
Requires a string id before constructing a repository cursor
Removes scattered service coercions in favor of explicit schema types and repository-level regression tests
